intro:
You will see a **text prompt** and **two AI-generated videos** made from it.

For **each quality criterion**, select the video with **higher quality** — 🅰️ A, 🅱️ B or 🟰 Tie:

1. 🖼️ **Image quality**
2. 🎞️ **Motion quality**
3. 🧍 **Subject–background consistency**
4. 🌍 **Realism**

Each criterion is explained on a real example in the next screens.
rules:
1. 🎓 **Demo pair** — already solved, with correct answers and explanations.

2. ✍️ **3 practice pairs** — you answer every metric yourself.

3. ⚡ Answers are checked **the moment you click**: ✅ correct → green, ❌ wrong → red + a hint and **one more try**.

4. 📝 Each pair ends with a **short comment** (min 30 characters).

5. 🏁 You pass when **all metrics on all 3 pairs** are correct.

metrics.title: Image Quality
metrics.question: Which video has sharper, cleaner image quality?
metrics.example: Look for blur, noise, distortion or watermarks — a watermark counts as bad quality.
metrics.hint: Re-check sharpness: blur, noise, distortion or a watermark all lower image quality.

metrics.title: Motion Quality
metrics.question: Which video has smoother, more natural motion?
metrics.example: Avoid videos with flickering, abrupt jumps, or almost no movement.
metrics.hint: Watch the movement again: flicker, abrupt jumps or a nearly frozen video score lower.

metrics.title: Subject–Background Consistency
metrics.question: Which video keeps the subject and background consistent throughout?
metrics.example: Penalize faces or backgrounds that morph or change unnaturally.
metrics.hint: Track faces and the background across the whole clip — morphing or warping means lower consistency.

metrics.title: Video Realism
metrics.question: Which video looks more real and natural?
metrics.example: Cartoonish looks or unnatural motion are less realistic.
metrics.hint: Judge how lifelike it looks — cartoonish rendering or impossible physics is less realistic.

comment_intro: Justify your selection in 2–3 sentences (at least 30 characters): what looked better and why.

demo.prompt: A man is sitting at a desk in front of a laptop.

reasons.image_quality: Video A stays sharp on the man and the laptop; Video B looks softer with more compression noise.
reasons.motion_quality: Video A moves smoothly; Video B has small jitters in the hands and screen.
reasons.subject_background_consistency: In Video A the desk and background stay stable; Video B warps slightly over time.
reasons.video_realism: Video A looks natural and lifelike; Video B has a faint, over-smoothed artificial look.

demo.comment: Video A is the clear winner: it stays sharper on the man and the laptop, moves more smoothly, and looks more natural, while Video B is softer and warps a little over time.

tasks.prompt: The video shows an astronaut in a spacesuit floating in space near a space station.
The video shows a group of piglets feeding from their mother in a pen.
A group of people are gathered around an older man who is lying down. They are all smiling and laughing together.
